description: Create a content slide on Europe near-term economic outlook with a two-column layout: a scenario comparison table on the left and a 'what to watch for' bullet list on the right.

header.headline: Sluggish growth is most likely across Europe, with manufacturing- and China-exposed economies at higher risk of renewed downturn, but some upside from AI/defense spending
header.sub: Europe near-term economic outlook and key scenarios

rows: Loss of growth momentum and rising vulnerability to recession due to combination of: Weak external demand; Fiscal tightening; Intense Chinese competition

Sluggish, uneven growth of ~1% as disinflation lifts real incomes, but core industrial weaknesses persist; Export-dependent and manufacturing-led economies lag, while services- and tourism-heavy ones hold steady
Growth strengthens toward ~1½–2% as key investments in economic security—green transition, defense, digital/AI infrastructure—accelerate and provide nascent productivity boost
Geopolitical shocks revive cost pressures; Fiscal tightening weighs on high-debt, demand-sensitive economies; China’s export surge further erodes manufacturing
Gradual rate cuts/easing of financial conditions; Real wages rise; capex and confidence muted; China competition keeps pressure on automotive, machinery, batteries, and solar manufacturing
Faster pass-through benefits of lower rates; Stronger execution of EU-funded and national investment programs; Global supply chain diversification benefits local manufacturing
Rising bankruptcies; Imperative on cost rationalization
Stable, low sales growth; Strong competition vs. China squeezes margins
Strong top-line growth; High returns on capex
